seoTitle: How to Copy Comments When Duplicating a Google Doc
metaDescription: Duplicate a Google Doc while keeping all comments and suggestions intact by checking one box in the Make a Copy dialog.
intro: Standard copy and paste only captures text, stripping out valuable feedback and comment threads. This guide shows how to use Google Docs' built-in file duplication feature to create a copy that preserves every comment alongside the text.

steps.heading: Open the File menu
steps.body: With your document open, click File in the top left to access the document management options.

steps.heading: Select Make a Copy
steps.body: Choose 'Make a Copy' from the dropdown to open the duplication dialog.

steps.heading: Name your new file
steps.body: Type a name for the cloned document and choose which Google Drive folder it should live in.

steps.heading: Check 'Copy comments and suggestions'
steps.body: Make sure this box is checked so your team's notes and feedback are carried over into the new file.

steps.heading: Create the copy and verify
steps.body: Click 'Make a Copy', wait for the redirect, and check the right margin of the new document to confirm the comments appear.

faq.question: Do resolved comments get copied too?
faq.answer: Resolved comments are generally excluded, since Google treats them as closed threads.

faq.question: Will the original commenter's name still show up?
faq.answer: Yes, attribution tags typically carry over so you can still see who made each comment.

faq.question: Can I copy comments using a shared link instead?
faq.answer: Yes, appending a specific parameter to a document's copy link can also force comments to transfer.

faq.question: Does this work for Google Sheets and Slides too?
faq.answer: Yes, the same 'Copy comments and suggestions' option appears when duplicating Sheets and Slides files.
